events.name: 2004 Indian Ocean Tsunami
events.origin: Off west coast of Sumatra, Indonesia
events.description: Deadliest tsunami in history. A Mw 9.1 megathrust earthquake generated waves up to 30m that struck 14 countries around the Indian Ocean.

events.name: 2011 Tōhoku Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 9.1 earthquake off Tōhoku sent 40m waves inland, triggering the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear meltdown.

events.name: 1960 Valdivia Tsunami
events.description: The most powerful earthquake ever recorded (Mw 9.5) generated a trans-Pacific tsunami reaching Hawaii, Japan, and the Philippines.

events.name: 1964 Alaska Tsunami
events.description: The Good Friday earthquake (Mw 9.2) triggered local tsunamis with run-up heights up to 67m in Valdez Inlet.

events.name: 1883 Krakatoa Tsunami
events.description: The cataclysmic eruption of Krakatoa volcano generated 37m tsunami waves that destroyed coastal towns across the Sunda Strait.

events.name: 1755 Lisbon Tsunami
events.description: One of history's deadliest earthquakes destroyed Lisbon. Tsunami waves up to 20m struck Portugal, Spain, and Morocco.

events.name: 1868 Arica Tsunami
events.description: A massive earthquake near Arica generated a destructive trans-Pacific tsunami reaching Hawaii, Japan, and New Zealand.

events.name: 1896 Meiji Sanriku Tsunami
events.description: A 'tsunami earthquake' generated disproportionately large 38m waves despite its moderate magnitude, devastating the Sanriku coast.

events.name: 1945 Makran Coast Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.1 earthquake in the Makran subduction zone generated a 13m tsunami that struck Balochistan and reached Mumbai.

events.name: 1941 Andaman Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 7.7 earthquake generated a local tsunami affecting the Andaman Islands and nearby coastal communities.

events.name: 2018 Sulawesi Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 7.5 strike-slip earthquake triggered an unexpected 11m tsunami that devastated Palu city.

events.name: 2018 Anak Krakatau Tsunami
events.description: A volcanic flank collapse of Anak Krakatau generated an unexpected 5m tsunami with no seismic warning.

events.name: 2005 Nias–Simeulue Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.6 aftershock of the 2004 event triggered tsunami warnings across the Indian Ocean.

events.name: 1958 Lituya Bay Mega-Tsunami
events.description: A rockslide-triggered mega-tsunami with the highest wave run-up ever recorded at 524m in the confined bay.

events.name: 1976 Moro Gulf Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.0 earthquake generated a 9m tsunami devastating coastal communities around Moro Gulf.

events.name: 1998 Papua New Guinea Tsunami
events.description: An undersea landslide triggered by a Mw 7.0 earthquake generated a 15m tsunami that destroyed Sissano Lagoon villages.

events.name: 2009 Samoa Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.1 earthquake generated a 14m tsunami that struck Samoa, American Samoa, and Tonga.

events.name: 2010 Chile Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.8 earthquake generated a destructive tsunami that devastated coastal Chile and sent waves across the Pacific.

events.name: 1933 Shōwa Sanriku Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.4 earthquake off Sanriku generated destructive 29m run-up waves, killing over 3,000 people.

events.name: 1946 Aleutian Tsunami
events.description: Generated 35m local waves destroying Scotch Cap Lighthouse and 17m waves that struck Hilo, Hawaii, leading to creation of the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center.

events.name: 1952 Kamchatka Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 9.0 earthquake generated an 18m tsunami that struck Kamchatka, the Kuril Islands, and reached Hawaii.

events.name: 1992 Flores Island Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 7.8 earthquake generated a 26m tsunami that devastated Flores Island's north coast.

events.name: 1993 Hokkaido Tsunami
events.description: A 31m tsunami struck Okushiri Island within 5 minutes of the earthquake, one of the fastest tsunami impacts recorded.

events.name: 2006 Java Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 7.7 'tsunami earthquake' generated 7m waves along Java's southern coast near Pangandaran.

events.name: 1929 Grand Banks Tsunami
events.description: An earthquake-triggered submarine landslide generated a 13m tsunami that struck the Burin Peninsula of Newfoundland.

events.name: 1960 Hilo Tsunami
events.origin: Trans-Pacific from Chile to Hilo, Hawaii
events.description: The Valdivia earthquake's tsunami crossed the Pacific and struck Hilo, Hawaii 15 hours later with 10m waves.

events.name: 2007 Solomon Islands Tsunami
events.description: A Mw 8.1 earthquake generated a 12m tsunami devastating low-lying villages in the western Solomon Islands.

events.name: 1908 Messina Tsunami
events.description: A devastating earthquake and 12m tsunami destroyed the cities of Messina and Reggio Calabria in southern Italy.

events.name: 1692 Port Royal Tsunami
events.description: An earthquake liquefied the ground and a tsunami swept over the notorious pirate city of Port Royal, sinking much of it.

events.name: 2004 Sri Lanka Tsunami Impact
events.description: The Indian Ocean tsunami struck Sri Lanka's eastern and southern coasts with 11m waves, killing over 35,000 people.
